blockingFindings.title: Required-field Zod defaults still mask malformed Epic 3 payloads in the production client
blockingFindings.evidence: The production client schemas still default required Epic 3 fields: `environment.lastCheckpointResult`, `process.controls`, `process.hasEnvironment`, and `processSourceReference.accessMode`. I also confirmed with a local tsx parse probe that payloads missing those fields still parse successfully as `null`, a 7-item default control set, `false`, and `read_only`.
blockingFindings.spec: The Round 2 brief requires no Zod defaults on required fields, and Epic 3 relies on durable/server truth for visible controls, checkpoint context, and source access mode.

nonblockingFindings.title: HydrationPlan fingerprint is still passed to adapters as an empty string
nonblockingFindings.evidence: `buildAdapterHydrationPlan` hard-codes `fingerprint: ''` even though the provider contract includes a fingerprint field. Current stale detection still works because Convex computes and stores `workingSetFingerprint` separately on env-state writes.
nonblockingFindings.spec: The provider contract in tech-design-server.md defines `HydrationPlan.fingerprint` as part of the adapter input; threading the real digest would keep the contract honest end-to-end.
